BUFFS.name: Buffs
BUFFS.summary: * Intensity buffs are more effective depending on how many stacks of the buff you have. These buffs typically are either permanent for the combat encounter or are consumed on the following turn. * Duration buffs last for as many turns as you have stacks of the buff. At the end of the turn, the buff will decrement by one stack. * Counter buffs activate when an event happens. Counter buffs typically lose a stack each time they activate. * Buffs that do not stack are typically gained from Power cards and last the entire combat encounter.

DEBUFFS.name: Debuffs
DEBUFFS.summary: * Intensity debuffs are more effective depending on how many stacks of the debuff you have. * Duration debuffs last for as many turns as you have stacks of the debuff. At the end of the turn, the debuff will decrement by one stack. * Debuffs that do not stack are typically gained from Power cards and last the entire combat encounter.

OSTY.name: Osty
OSTY.summary: Osty is a mechanic used by the . Osty is the animated left hand of the Necrobinder. Osty innately has  while he is alive. The Necrobinder has Attacks that cause Osty to Attack, which have no effect if Osty is dead when they are played. When Osty attacks, he does not consider any buffs or debuffs on the player, such as  or . Osty cannot receive any buff or debuff aside from . He cannot be directly targeted by an enemy, a potion, or another player. When the player would receive any unblocked attack damage, the damage is first deducted from Osty's HP. If the damage is greater than Osty's current

POTIONS.name: Potions
POTIONS.summary: Potions are single-use consumables that persist across floors. Most potions provide a temporary boost that can only be used in a single encounter, and thus can only be used in combat. The only potions that can be used outside of combat are: ,  (only when used against the ), , and  (Ironclad only). Potions are stored in Potion Slots, of which you start with 3 (). The only ways to increase the amount of slots you have is to obtain , , or . Using a potion does not cost  and does not count as playing a card. Some potions are exclusive to a single character. Each character has 3 unique potions, wit

REST_SITES.name: Rest Sites
REST_SITES.summary: for all possible rooms.}} Rest Sites are a standard , appearing multiple times per . The last room before each boss in Acts 1, 2, and 3 is always a rest site. The player must choose one of the available options to prepare for higher floors before proceeding. If one of the standard options is unavailable for some reason, it will be greyed out.

SOVEREIGN_BLADE.name: Sovereign Blade
SOVEREIGN_BLADE.summary: is a 2 (1 if upgraded)  cost    Card available to the . At its base value, it deals 10 damage to a target. The  has a lot of dedicated support cards for the .

TRANSFORM.name: Transform
TRANSFORM.summary: Transforming a card acts as removing that card and adding a random eligible card to the deck. This means it will trigger all the effects of both Card removals and Card additions.

UPGRADE.name: Upgrade
UPGRADE.summary: Upgrading a card improves its effects or decreases its cost, adds a + to the end of its name, and turns its name  as well as the cost, if it was lowered as a result of Upgrading it. A card can only be upgraded once and all  and  cards cannot be upgraded.
